stem: Select all of the following that are error MITIGATION techniques for the Estimator — methods that post-process results from an ensemble of circuits to reduce bias in expectation values (as opposed to error suppression, which prevents errors before/during execution).

explanation.correct: Error mitigation runs an ensemble of related circuits and post-processes their outputs to reduce bias in expectation values. TREX mitigates readout error, ZNE amplifies noise then extrapolates to the zero-noise limit, and PEC expresses the ideal result as a quasi-probabilistic combination of noisy circuits (unbiased, but with sampling overhead). All three are mitigation.

distractors.A: Dynamical decoupling is error SUPPRESSION: it inserts pulse sequences on idle qubits to prevent decoherence before it happens — it does not post-process results.
distractors.D: Choosing a higher transpiler optimization_level reduces gate count during compilation; it is a circuit-optimization setting, not a runtime error-mitigation post-processing method.
